title: Soluble and Insoluble Substances Lab
subject: Science
summary: The strongest current portfolio example: a visible, quantitative experiment in which students predict, measure, stir, observe, and reconcile every gram of material.
strengths: Real mass balance, saturation, temperature, settling, suspension and oil-separation behaviour
Physical beaker, 200 mL water, five materials, measured scoop and molecular evidence view
33 packaged narration clips plus singleton sound control prevent overlapping instructions
risks: Most apparatus is procedural rather than authored high-detail laboratory assets
No signed Quest headset acceptance or classroom outcome study
Internal QA maturity means teacher workflow and multi-student robustness remain unproven
action: Prioritise for the first controlled school pilot after Quest performance and teacher-facilitation acceptance.

title: Plant Pollination & Growth Cycle
subject: Biology
summary: A well-structured investigation rather than a passive plant tour, with treatment/control reasoning and the complete journey from flower parts to germination.
strengths: Eight evidence-gated stages distinguish pollination, fertilisation, seed formation and germination
Treatment/control experiment and misconception feedback support causal reasoning
Mapped materials, garden tools, scale disclosure, eight narration clips and 29 focused tests
risks: Live automated entry cleared to a flat green frame, so cross-device visual reliability needs acceptance testing
Asset richness is primarily procedural and does not yet match premium botanical simulations
No classroom learning-gain or teacher-usage evidence
action: Run a Quest/browser rendering acceptance pass, then pilot as the second flagship science experience.

title: States of Matter Particle Lab
subject: Chemistry and Physics
summary: A compact, conceptually strong particle laboratory that makes invisible spacing and motion observable while linking heat changes to phase behaviour.
strengths: Authored solid, liquid and gas reference states with tested heat mapping
Instanced particles efficiently show spacing, cohesion and motion
Prediction/observation flow, representation disclosure and four packaged stage narrations
risks: Only four stages limit repeated investigation and transfer
Real-time audit remained at the mode-selection presentation after automated entry
Particle representation is instructional, not molecularly literal, and needs explicit teacher framing
action: Add a comparison/transfer challenge and complete browser plus Quest entry-flow acceptance before pilot.

title: Electric Circuits & Resistance (Ohm's Law)
subject: Physics
summary: A defensible interactive circuit model that connects physical components, current flow, resistance changes and prediction through Ohm's law.
strengths: Verified electrical truth model for open/closed circuits and V=IR
Learners manipulate physical battery, switch, bulb and resistor objects
Mapped PBR presentation, four packaged stage narrations and browser/VR controls
risks: Only four stages and limited component variety constrain experimentation depth
Automated live entry remained on the browser/VR choice presentation
No measurement uncertainty, multimeter workflow or classroom validation
action: Extend with meter reading and fault-finding tasks, then run end-to-end controller acceptance.

title: Introduction to Money
subject: Mathematics
summary: A colourful, age-appropriate Money Town journey that moves from identifying Indian currency to comparing values and making simple purchases.
strengths: Five-stage progression covers coins, notes, value comparison, shopping and memory
Large targets, simple prompts, comfort mode and browser/VR navigation suit young learners
Live child-facing scene rendered cleanly and dedicated lesson tests protect scoring logic
risks: Narration depends on browser speech rather than consistent packaged voice assets
Procedural assets look playful but not premium or culturally detailed
Currency authenticity and classroom usability have not been externally reviewed
action: Record a child-friendly voice pack and complete teacher review of currency wording and purchase tasks.

title: Preposition Adventure
subject: English
summary: A strong use of spatial XR for language learning: students place objects, connect position to meaning, and practise simple location sentences.
strengths: Placement interaction makes abstract position words concrete
Five stages include guided practice, sentence prompts and memory checks
Bright scene, large controls, comfort mode and clean live rendering
risks: Browser text-to-speech cannot guarantee consistent pronunciation or classroom voice quality
Automated scoring emphasises placement more than spoken-language evidence
Procedural environments repeat the visual language of other early-years lessons
action: Add packaged narration and teacher-reviewed spoken-response prompts before a language-learning pilot.

title: Effects of Force on Motion and Shape
subject: Physics
summary: The physics foundation is stronger than the presentation: learners push, redirect, brake and deform objects through a deterministic motion model.
strengths: Fixed-step tests demonstrate inertia, braking, directional force and bounded collisions
Six stages connect starting, stopping, speeding, direction change and deformation
Direct manipulation provides meaningful cause-and-effect feedback
risks: Post-entry production audit produced a blank headless frame despite a mounted canvas
No packaged narration and limited visual asset richness
Deformation examples need closer visual realism and headset acceptance
action: Treat visual rendering acceptance and richer deformation feedback as release gates before pilot.

title: Sources of Food Sorting Lab
subject: Science and Biology
summary: A clear, usable sorting experience with unusually complete spoken feedback, but limited visual depth and comparatively thin dedicated test coverage.
strengths: Students classify eight foods as plant, animal or fungal and receive misconception feedback
36 packaged clips cover stages, item clues, correct and incorrect placements
Live tabletop and controller-accessible category platforms rendered without errors
risks: Simplified food tokens and tabletop make the experience feel closer to a 3D worksheet
Only two dedicated viewer regression tests despite many feedback branches
Little transfer beyond the authored item set
action: Improve food asset recognisability, add transfer items, and expand scoring regression coverage.

title: Acids, Bases & Neutralisation
subject: Chemistry
summary: A sound learning sequence for indicators and neutralisation, with a tested chemistry model but a presentation that still reads as an early procedural prototype.
strengths: Five stages connect litmus, universal indicator, pH and neutralisation
Tested model maps acid/neutral/base states and indicator colours
Learners act on an experiment bench rather than only answer questions
risks: Post-entry production audit produced a blank headless frame despite a mounted canvas
No packaged narration or high-detail glassware/liquid assets
No explicit laboratory-safety assessment or classroom validation
action: Resolve cross-browser rendering, add laboratory-quality materials and include a safety decision stage.

title: Colour Adventure
subject: Art and Foundational Learning
summary: A cheerful early-years colour experience with good visual appeal and gated memory activities, weakened by release-governance and narration gaps.
strengths: Bright rainbow classroom and colour worlds are immediately understandable
Five-stage recognition, finding and memory journey uses large age-appropriate controls
Live scene rendered cleanly with comfort and restart support
risks: Launchable publicly but absent from the canonical 12-module release registry
Relies on browser speech instead of a consistent child-friendly voice
Scientific/content review evidence is lighter than for curriculum-registered modules
action: Fix release registration first, then package narration and conduct an early-years usability review.

title: The Breathing Process in Human
subject: Biology
summary: A well-scoped six-stage physiology lesson covering both mechanics and gas exchange, but current production rendering and asset evidence are not yet convincing enough for pilot use.
strengths: Traces airway, lungs, diaphragm, chest volume and alveolar gas exchange
Action/evidence gates prevent passive progression
Low comfort-risk design and shared browser/VR framework
risks: Post-entry production audit produced a blank headless frame despite a mounted canvas
Procedural anatomy and no packaged narration reduce realism and consistency
Only four dedicated experience tests and no external anatomy review evidence
action: Prioritise visible respiratory mechanics, packaged narration and anatomy review before headset trials.

title: Introduction to the Digestive System
subject: Biology
summary: The lesson design is ambitious and thorough, but the deployed visual experience is materially degraded because every authored organ model request fails and falls back to procedural geometry.
strengths: Ten-stage food journey covers organs, peristalsis, accessory organs, absorption, recap and healthy habits
Strong accessible HTML controls, subtitles, comfort mode and Quest controller design
21 focused tests protect pathway, evidence gates and assessment scoring
risks: All 11 production GLB requests returned 404 during audit
No packaged narration; browser voice quality varies by device
Procedural fallback cannot deliver the intended body-scale anatomical realism
action: Block pilot release until GLB delivery is fixed and every organ is visually accepted on browser and Quest.

title: Solar System: Gravity's Orchestra
subject: Science, Physics and Geography
summary: The portfolio's deepest conceptual and scientific design is currently unusable in production: the live route pauses before mounting the 3D world.
strengths: Eight-stage mission confronts orbital-speed, hottest-planet, scale and comet misconceptions
Verified Kepler-law, orbital-period, scale-blend and comet models with 35 dedicated tests
Credited local textures for the Sun and all eight planets plus a free observatory mode
risks: Production stops with 'roundRect is not a function' and mounts no canvas
Two production resources returned 403 in the focused runtime inspection
No packaged narration and medium comfort risk remain unvalidated on Quest
action: Treat as a release blocker: fix the runtime and resource failures, redeploy, then repeat the full visual/audio/controller audit.
